current_judgment: The remaining ingestion audit settings/layout boilerplate is materially cleaner now. The file still contains plenty of test data, but the confusing repeated scaffolding is no longer hand-expanded across the suite.
key_evidence: tests/test_ingestion_audit.py now has helper coverage for both part1 and corpus-lane settings: _audit_settings(...) handles the default part1 contract and _corpus_settings(...) handles the multiversion corpus lane.
All remaining settings = SimpleNamespace(...) blocks in tests/test_ingestion_audit.py were removed: 13 -> 0.

The last real manual root layout starts for manifests/part1-or-corpus/raw_html plus extra directory creation were collapsed from 5 repeated blocks to helper-based setup; the only remaining 'manifests = root / "manifests"' occurrence is inside the helper definition itself.
File size dropped from 98,587 bytes to 95,057 bytes while pytest coverage remained intact.

The repo hotspot list is clearer after this cut: tests/test_ingestion_audit.py is still the largest tracked test file, followed by tests/test_app_viewers.py and tests/test_retrieval_runtime.py, while product-side concentration remains in src/play_book_studio/ingestion/curated_gold.py and src/play_book_studio/app/source_books.py.
validation_results: npm --prefix presentation-ui run test -> 3 files, 9 tests passed
npm --prefix presentation-ui run build -> passed with existing vite chunk-size warning
post-validation hygiene cleanup -> removed 6 junk targets / 1,089,678 bytes / workspace_junk_count=0
next_action: The next cleanup should move from tests to product code: start with src/play_book_studio/ingestion/curated_gold.py and src/play_book_studio/app/source_books.py, because those two files now dominate the same 'slow to reason about, easy to break' pattern on the runtime side.
